file_name: VirtuosoSurgicalInc_20191227_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11933379_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_License Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Minimum Annual Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU minimum annual royalties as set forth in Exhibit A. These minimum annual royalties shall be due within thirty (30) days of each anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE beginning with the first anniversary. Running royalties accrued under Paragraph 4.3 and paid to JHU during the one year period preceding an anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE shall be credited against the minimum annual royalties due on that anniversary date.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Minimum Annual Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU minimum annual royalties as set forth in Exhibit A. These minimum annual royalties shall be due within forty-five (45) days of each anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE beginning with the first anniversary. Running royalties accrued under Paragraph 4.3 and paid to JHU during the one year period preceding an anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE shall be credited against the minimum annual royalties due on that anniversary date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the payment deadline for minimum annual royalties. Paragraph 4.2 now states that these royalties are due within forty-five (45) days of each anniversary, conflicting with the general payment terms specified in Paragraph 4.3, which mandates royalty payments within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter. This discrepancy creates ambiguity regarding which deadline applies to the minimum annual royalties, potentially leading to disputes over late payment penalties or breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Running Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU, a running royalty as set forth in Exhibit A, for each LICENSED PRODUCT(S) sold and each LICENSED SERVICE(S) provided, based upon NET SALES and NET SERVICE REVENUES respectively, for the term of this Agreement. Such payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter following first commercial sale of LICENSED PRODUCT or providing LICENSED SERVICES. All non-US taxes related to LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and LICENSED SERVICE(S) sold under this Agreement shall be paid by Company and shall not be deducted from royalty or other payments due to JHU.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Paragraph 4.2 changes the payment deadline for minimum annual royalties from thirty (30) days to forty-five (45) days. This directly contradicts Paragraph 4.3, which states that running royalty payments are due within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter. This inconsistency in payment deadlines creates a clear contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, leading to uncertainty about which deadline should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: 4.5 Late Payments: In the event that any payment due hereunder is not made when due, the payment shall accrue interest beginning on the tenth day following the due date thereof, calculated at the annual rate of six percent (6%), the interest being compounded on the last day of each calendar year. Each such royalty payment when made shall be accompanied by all interest so accrued. Said interest and the payment and acceptance thereof shall not negate or waive the right of JHU to seek any other remedy, legal or equitable, to which it may be entitled because of the delinquency of any payment.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5 Late Payments: In the event that any payment due hereunder is not made when due, the payment shall accrue interest beginning on the sixtieth day following the due date thereof, calculated at the annual rate of six percent (6%), the interest being compounded on the last day of each calendar year. Each such royalty payment when made shall be accompanied by all interest so accrued. Said interest and the payment and acceptance thereof shall not negate or waive the right of JHU to seek any other remedy, legal or equitable, to which it may be entitled because of the delinquency of any payment.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the commencement of interest accrual on late payments. Changing the start date of interest accrual from the tenth day to the sixtieth day directly conflicts with the thirty-day payment terms outlined in sections 4.2 and 4.3. The inconsistency creates ambiguity regarding the acceptable grace period for payments and the enforcement of late payment penalties, as one clause implies a shorter grace period (ten days) than the newly introduced sixty-day period, creating uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Minimum Annual Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU minimum annual royalties as set forth in Exhibit A. These minimum annual royalties shall be due within forty-five (45) days of each anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE beginning with the first anniversary. Running royalties accrued under Paragraph 4.3 and paid to JHU during the one year period preceding an anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE shall be credited against the minimum annual royalties due on that anniversary date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the grace period for late payments from 10 days to 60 days. This directly contradicts section 4.2, which requires minimum annual royalties to be paid within 45 days of each anniversary of the EFFECTIVE DATE. The introduction of a 60-day grace period for interest accrual on late payments creates a conflict with the pre-existing 45-day payment deadline, resulting in an in-text contradiction regarding the permissible timeframe for fulfilling payment obligations and the enforcement of late payment penalties.

perturbation.original_text: 4.3 Running Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU, a running royalty as set forth in Exhibit A, for each LICENSED PRODUCT(S) sold and each LICENSED SERVICE(S) provided, based upon NET SALES and NET SERVICE REVENUES respectively, for the term of this Agreement. Such payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter following first commercial sale of LICENSED PRODUCT or providing LICENSED SERVICES. All non-US taxes related to LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and LICENSED SERVICE(S) sold under this Agreement shall be paid by Company and shall not be deducted from royalty or other payments due to JHU.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 Running Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU, a running royalty as set forth in Exhibit A, for each LICENSED PRODUCT(S) sold and each LICENSED SERVICE(S) provided, based upon NET SALES and NET SERVICE REVENUES respectively, for the term of this Agreement. Such payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter following first commercial sale of LICENSED PRODUCT or providing LICENSED SERVICES. All non-US taxes related to LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and LICENSED SERVICE(S) sold under this Agreement shall be paid by Company and shall be deducted from royalty or other payments due to JHU.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction regarding the handling of non-US taxes related to Licensed Products and Services. Paragraph 4.3 originally stated that all non-US taxes should be paid by the Company and not deducted from royalty payments to JHU. The modified text now states that these taxes should be deducted from royalty payments. This directly contradicts the original clause, introducing ambiguity about which party bears the burden of these taxes and directly impacting the net royalty amount JHU receives. This could lead to disputes over royalty calculations and financial obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Running Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU, a running royalty as set forth in Exhibit A, for each LICENSED PRODUCT(S) sold and each LICENSED SERVICE(S) provided, based upon NET SALES and NET SERVICE REVENUES respectively, for the term of this Agreement. Such payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter following first commercial sale of LICENSED PRODUCT or providing LICENSED SERVICES. All non-US taxes related to LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and LICENSED SERVICE(S) sold under this Agreement shall be paid by Company and shall be deducted from royalty or other payments due to JHU.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Paragraph 4.3 directly contradicts the original text by changing 'shall not be deducted' to 'shall be deducted' regarding non-US taxes from royalty payments to JHU. This creates a clear and impactful financial inconsistency within the same paragraph, directly impacting the amount JHU receives and creating a situation where both versions cannot be simultaneously true. The Contradicted Text is in the same location as the changed text; however, the explanation clearly shows that the changed text directly contradicts the original text.
